current_suggestion.channel_name: Odyssey of the Mind (William MacAskill / 80,000 Hours)
current_suggestion.pitch: Your philosophy and mental models interests are well-served on the Stoic/classical side, but 80,000 Hours steelmans the rigorous analytical ethics angle you're missing — effective altruism, moral uncertainty, and how to reason carefully about what actually matters. Their long-form interviews (often 2-3 hours) with philosophers, economists, and scientists treat ethics empirically rather than dogmatically, which pairs well with your Munger/Kahneman mental models interest. The depth rivals Lex Fridman but stays closer to philosophy and decision theory rather than tech celebrity.

history: TEDx Talks – Russ Roberts (EconTalk host solo appearances)
EP Wealth Advisors (with Ben Felix-style rigor — try 'The Plain Bagel' instead)
Odyssey of the Mind (William MacAskill / 80,000 Hours)
